question.text: Under the new tax regime for FY 2025-26, the maximum surcharge rate applicable on total income (other than capital gains and dividends) is:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under the new tax regime, the maximum surcharge on income tax (for income other than capital gains under Section 111A/112/112A and dividends) is capped at 25%, even for income exceeding Rs. 5 crore. This is a key difference from the old regime, where the highest surcharge rate is 37% for income above Rs. 5 crore. **The Trap:** 37% is the maximum surcharge under the old regime — candidates switching between regimes may confuse the two. 15% is the surcharge cap on capital gains and dividend income. 10% is the surcharge for the Rs. 50L-1Cr bracket under both regimes. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-B specifically tests differences between old and new regime for investment advisory practice. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 25% surcharge cap under the new regime can make it significantly advantageous for ultra-HNI clients (income >5Cr) — their effective rate drops from 42.744% (old) to 39% (new) on the top slab.
